what_manipulated: reminder of physical cleansing
what_measured: political attitude conservatism and moral judgment severity
experiment: Units reported political attitudes and moral judgments in response to scenarios. Study 1 conducted in public setting with cleansing reminder manipulation; Study 2 in laboratory with same manipulation. Control condition received no reminder. Design tested whether physical purity activation shifts moral judgment and political orientation.

what_manipulated: motivation framing for a good deed (religious vs. other reasons)
what_measured: perceived morality of the act and perceived responsibility of the actor
experiment: Six experiments (N=1,078 total) presented units with descriptions of identical helping actions attributed to religious versus non-religious motivations. Units rated the perceived morality and responsibility of the actor. Experiment 6 tested whether responsibility mediated the effect of motivation framing on morality perception.

what_manipulated: framing direction of comparison (more-than vs. less-than statements) and cognitive fluency expectation
what_measured: statement preference, agreement, and truth acceptance
experiment: Seven studies tested whether logically equivalent comparative statements framed as 'more than' versus 'less than' produce different responses. Study 1 measured statement usage frequency. Studies 2-3 measured preference ratings. Studies 4-5 measured agreement. Study 6 measured truth judgments. Study 7 manipulated fluency expectations to test the cognitive fluency mechanism.

what_manipulated: roundedness of price numbers (rounded vs. nonrounded) and decision context (feeling-driven vs. cognition-driven)
what_measured: product evaluations and purchase preferences
experiment: Five studies presented units with products at prices that were either rounded or nonrounded. Designs varied decision context (feeling-driven or cognition-driven purchases). Units evaluated products and indicated purchase intentions. Mediation analyses tested whether 'feeling right' fit between price roundedness and decision context drove evaluations.

what_manipulated: philosophies of life endorsed by units (no manipulation; observational)
what_measured: well-being, civic engagement, and optimism about the future
experiment: Three studies asked units binary choice questions about whether life is short or long and easy or hard. Units' endorsement of life philosophies was correlated with self-reported well-being, civic engagement, and optimism. Comparison focused on short-hard versus long-easy philosophy endorsement.

what_manipulated: biological sex and sexual orientation (measured, not manipulated)
what_measured: mating psychology dimensions predicted by sexual selection theory
experiment: Scales measuring mating psychology were administered to heterosexual and homosexual units of both sexes. Analysis examined whether sex differences in mating psychology scale scores were consistent with sexual selection theory predictions. Correlation structure of scale intercorrelations was compared across groups.

what_manipulated: color of stimulus (red vs. other colors: achromatic and chromatic)
what_measured: attractiveness and sexual desirability ratings
experiment: Five experiments presented images of women to male units, varying the color context (red, achromatic, or other chromatic colors). Units rated attractiveness and sexual desirability. Control conditions tested whether red influenced perception of likeability, kindness, or intelligence. Awareness was probed post-hoc.

what_manipulated: sex ratio in the operating environment (implied through scenarios or ecological context; measured)
what_measured: diversification of resource allocation across risky financial options
experiment: Four studies presented units with financial decision tasks (lottery, stock, retirement, research funding). Conditions varied the implied sex ratio (favorable vs. unfavorable). Units allocated resources across options or concentrated in high-risk/high-return options. Analysis compared diversification patterns across sex-ratio conditions.

what_manipulated: examples provided for word-referent learning (varied by number and category structure)
what_measured: generalization of word meaning to novel referents
experiment: Three experiments taught units novel words with one or few positive examples. Units generalized meanings to novel referents. Bayesian model predictions were tested against observed generalization patterns. Comparison with hypothesis-elimination and associative-learning accounts evaluated model fit.

what_manipulated: prior experience with answers (old vs. new questions) and presentation format (answer presented with or separately from question)
what_measured: accuracy of predictions about what others know
experiment: Two experiments asked units to answer general knowledge questions then estimate peers' knowledge. Experiment 1 compared estimates for questions with prior exposure versus new questions. Experiment 2 blocked experience cues by presenting answers with questions. Relative accuracy of discrimination between easy and hard questions for others was analyzed.

what_manipulated: desirability of objects relevant to internal goal states (water, money, favorable feedback vs. control objects)
what_measured: perceived distance of objects from the observer
experiment: Five experiments presented units with objects varying in desirability based on current goals (thirst, win opportunity, feedback preference). Units reported perceived distance or performed motor actions toward objects. Verbal reports and action measures (beanbag throwing accuracy) indicated biased distance perception for desirable versus undesirable objects.

what_manipulated: race and age of faces in priming task (Black vs. White, child vs. adult faces)
what_measured: speed and accuracy of threat identification
experiment: Four experiments presented faces (varying race and age) as primes before threat-categorization tasks. Units categorized threatening and nonthreatening stimuli and words. Response times and error rates were compared across prime conditions. Process-dissociation analyses distinguished automatic from intentional components.

what_manipulated: eating disorder status and body-weight status (bulimic, obese, normal; measured)
what_measured: psychological distress, depression, anxiety, and impulsivity
experiment: Units in three groups (bulimic N=15, obese N=15, normal N=15) completed standardized assessments: MMPI, SCL-90, BDI, and body-image evaluation. Group comparisons assessed differences in anxiety, depression, neuroticism, and impulsivity.

what_manipulated: psychiatric diagnosis and suicide attempt history (measured)
what_measured: presence or absence of suicide attempt and risk factor profile
experiment: 347 consecutive units admitted to psychiatric hospital completed structured interviews and assessments spanning multiple domains: psychiatric diagnosis, lifetime suicidal acts, aggression and impulsivity, depression/psychosis severity, family history, substance use. Group comparison between suicide attempters and non-attempters identified risk factors generalizable across diagnoses.

what_manipulated: none; optimism, control, and self-esteem measured as predictors
what_measured: college adjustment and academic performance over time
experiment: 672 units completed baseline measures of optimism, control, and self-esteem. Coping strategies and mood were assessed. Adjustment outcomes and grades were measured at follow-up (2 years later). Longitudinal mediation models tested whether coping methods mediated effects of traits on adjustment and grades.

what_manipulated: none; positive and negative affect measured as predictors
what_measured: broad-minded coping strategy use and emotional well-being over time
experiment: 138 units completed affect and coping measures at two timepoints 5 weeks apart. Regression analyses tested reciprocal prospective prediction: initial positive affect predicting change in broad-minded coping, and vice versa. Mediation analyses examined serial reciprocal enhancement.

what_manipulated: none; spirituality constructs measured
what_measured: latent dimensions of spirituality and relation to Big Five personality factors
experiment: Study 1 (N=534) conducted factor analysis on 11 spirituality measures to identify latent structure. Study 2 (N=938) replicated structure and developed 98-item Expressions of Spirituality Inventory. Correlations examined relationships between spirituality dimensions and Big Five personality factors from NEO-PI-R.

what_manipulated: none; personality and identity measured as predictors across 31 years
what_measured: patterns of well-being (environmental mastery and personal growth) at age 60
experiment: 111 units from longitudinal study assessed at age 21, 43, and 60 on personality, identity processes, and self-control. Cluster analysis identified three well-being patterns (Achievers, Conservers, Seekers) on well-being scales. Profiles on maturity criteria were compared across patterns. Mediation tested whether age-43 identity mediated age-21 personality effects on age-60 well-being pattern.

what_manipulated: none; Big Five personality measured
what_measured: complex behaviors (alcohol consumption, academic performance, etc.)
experiment: Units completed Big Five personality measures using three different instruments (NEO-FFI, NEO-PI-R, Five-Factor Nonverbal Inventory) across two independent samples (N=276 and N=142). Personality scores were correlated with criterion behaviors. Consistency in prediction across samples and instruments was examined.

what_manipulated: none; personality traits measured via self and partner ratings
what_measured: relationship satisfaction
experiment: 74 married and 136 dating couples provided self-rated and partner-rated Big Five and affectivity measures. Target's satisfaction was correlated with four rating sources: target self-rating, partner-rating of target, partner self-rating, and target-rating of partner. Prediction of satisfaction variance from trait combinations was examined across couple types.
